# `dist/windows/` — committed launcher templates

Version-agnostic `.cmd` launchers for unidrive on Windows. Filed under
**UD-718**: the pre-2026-05 `%LOCALAPPDATA%\unidrive\unidrive.cmd` and
`unidrive-jfr.cmd` were hand-rolled with a hard-coded
`unidrive-0.0.0-greenfield.jar` reference that went stale at the
0.0.1 cutover.

## What's here

| File | Purpose |
| ---- | ------- |
| `unidrive.cmd` | Plain launcher, mirrors `unidrive.ps1` semantics for users who call from `cmd.exe`. |
| `unidrive-jfr.cmd` | Same as above plus a minimal `-XX:StartFlightRecording=…` flag. For richer profiling (sha-tagged filenames, `settings=profile`, HeapDumpOnOOM) prefer [`scripts/dev/unidrive-jfr.ps1`](../../scripts/dev/unidrive-jfr.ps1). |

Both pick the newest `unidrive-*.jar` next to themselves (by mtime,
`dir /b /od` last entry) and explicitly skip `unidrive-mcp-*.jar` so a
co-located MCP fat-jar doesn't shadow the CLI.

## How to install

```cmd
copy dist\windows\unidrive.cmd      %LOCALAPPDATA%\unidrive\
copy dist\windows\unidrive-jfr.cmd  %LOCALAPPDATA%\unidrive\
```

After `./gradlew :app:cli:deploy` from `core/`, the JAR in
`%LOCALAPPDATA%\unidrive\` updates and the launchers keep working
without any further edits.

## Why aren't these regenerated by `:app:cli:deploy`?

UD-718 was fixed scripts-only — the deploy task in
`core/app/cli/build.gradle.kts` was out of scope. A follow-up could
fold these into `deployWindows()` so a fresh install picks them up
without the manual `copy` step. See UD-718 for the trail.

## See also

- [`scripts/dev/unidrive-jfr.ps1`](../../scripts/dev/unidrive-jfr.ps1) — production-grade JFR wrapper (recommended).
- [`scripts/dev/unidrive-jfr.sh`](../../scripts/dev/unidrive-jfr.sh) — Linux / Git-Bash sibling.
